items.name: 4-7-8 Breathing
items.description: Inhale 4, hold 7, exhale 8 — extended exhale stimulates vagus nerve
items.difficulty: Medium — the hold can be challenging at first
items.key_mechanism: Extended exhale activates parasympathetic nervous system via vagus nerve

items.name: Box Breathing
items.description: Inhale 4, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4 — equal intervals create calm rhythm
items.best_for: High-pressure work moments, production incidents, focus
items.difficulty: Easy — equal counts are simple to remember
items.key_mechanism: Rhythmic breathing anchors attention and activates parasympathetic response

items.name: Physiological Sigh
items.description: Double inhale through nose (long + short), then long exhale through mouth
items.best_for: Immediate calm — fastest known breathing technique
items.difficulty: Very easy — can be done anywhere without being noticed
items.key_mechanism: Double inhale maximizes lung CO2 offloading, fast parasympathetic activation

items.name: Diaphragmatic (Belly) Breathing
items.description: Deep breaths that expand the belly, not the chest — engages diaphragm fully
items.difficulty: Easy to learn, requires practice to make natural
items.key_mechanism: Full diaphragm engagement maximizes oxygen exchange and vagus nerve stimulation

items.name: Alternate Nostril Breathing (Nadi Shodhana)
items.description: Breathe through one nostril at a time, alternating with finger placement
items.key_mechanism: Balances sympathetic and parasympathetic activation, improves focus

comparisons.insight: For developers, the most practical techniques are Box Breathing (work moments) and Physiological Sigh (immediate calm). Use 4-7-8 for sleep. Practice one technique daily until it's automatic, then add others.

keyInsight: The fastest stress relief technique is the Physiological Sigh (double inhale + long exhale) — one breath can calm you down. For work, use Box Breathing. For sleep, use 4-7-8. Practice daily so they're automatic under pressure.
